identity.content:
# Identity

## Who I Am
[Your name, role (e.g., full-stack developer, CTO, indie hacker), years of experience, areas of specialization]

## Tech Stack
- **Languages:** [Your primary languages]
- **Frameworks:** [Your frameworks and libraries]
- **Tools:** [Your development tools, IDE, version control]
- **Cloud/Infra:** [Your cloud providers, hosting, CI/CD]

## Voice & Tone
[How should AI communicate with you? Examples: direct and technical, explain like a colleague, use code examples, skip basics, be verbose, etc.]

## Values
[What principles guide your work? Examples: clean code, testing, documentation, shipping fast, simplicity, etc.]

instructions.content:
# Instructions

## Language
[What language should AI respond in?]

## Code Style
[Your coding conventions — naming, formatting, patterns, comments style]

## Stack Preferences
[Framework-specific rules — e.g., which ORM to use, component patterns, CSS approach]

## Things to Avoid
[What should AI never do? Anti-patterns, deprecated approaches, unwanted behaviors]

context.content:
# Context

## Active Projects
[List your current projects with brief descriptions, tech stack, and status]

## Current Priorities
[What are you focused on right now? Any blockers?]

## Deadlines
[Upcoming milestones or deliverables with dates]

## Team
[Who else is involved? Their roles and responsibilities]
